evals.prompt: Write the UI microcopy for the states the user named.
evals.expected_output: UI copy where error states are blame-free (describe the situation, never fault the user) and every error and empty state offers a next action (no dead ends); buttons/CTAs are verb+object matching the user's goal (not 'Submit'/'OK'); labels are concise, front-loaded, and terminology-consistent (one term per concept). Tone fits each context (calm and plain in an error, warmer in success) while the product voice stays constant. It shapes words only — no visual/layout decisions and no documentation prose.
assertions: Error copy is blame-free — describes the situation, never faults the user
Every error and empty state offers a next action — no dead-end copy
Buttons / CTAs are verb + object matching the user's goal, not generic ('Submit' / 'OK')
Labels are concise, front-loaded, and terminology-consistent (one term per concept)
Tone fits the context (calm in errors, warmer in success) while the voice stays constant
Shapes words only — no visual/layout decisions and no documentation prose
